type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: In 2023, no new sulfuric acid waste required disposal due to minimal generation volumes. The recorded quantity (0.3255 tonnes) reflects the off-site disposal of residual material accumulated from previous years, including sulfuric acid collected during clean-up and repairs to equipment within the containment berm. This material was transferred to GFL Environmental’s facility at 100 Cory Road, Saskatoon, SK, for chemical treatment via neutralization.

type.en: Reason why substance was added to the report
type.fr: Raison pour laquelle la substance a été ajoutée au rapport

comments.comment: Sulfuric acid (CAS 7664-93-9) was added to the 2023 report due to the off-site disposal of residual material collected during clean-up and maintenance activities within the containment berm. This material was transported to GFL Environmental’s facility in Saskatoon for neutralization. The primary source of sulfuric acid at the facility remains line purging from delivery operations.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The 2024 stack test data for RTO-A and Boiler No.1 were reviewed in consultation with SRC. The RTO-A condensable PM value appears consistent with historical results and was not flagged. However, the filterable PM for RTO-A in 2024 (PM-5) may be considered an outlier due to potential sample contamination, as noted by visual evidence of yellow material and abnormal deviation from historical values. Similarly, Boiler No.1 2024 data (PM1) is potentially contaminated and may be excluded from trend evaluations. It is also noted that 2024 experienced fewer days with precipitation /snow during the stack testing period, which could contribute to higher measured particulate concentrations relative to previous years.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The 2024 stack test data for RTO-A and Boiler No.1 were reviewed in consultation with SRC. The RTO-A condensable PM value appears consistent with historical results and was not flagged. However, the filterable PM for RTO-A in 2024 (PM-5) may be considered an outlier due to potential sample contamination, as noted by visual evidence of yellow material and abnormal deviation from historical values. Similarly, Boiler No.1 2024 data (PM1) is potentially contaminated and may be excluded from trend evaluations. It is also noted that 2024 experienced fewer days with precipitation/snow during the stack testing period, which could contribute to higher measured particulate concentrations relative to previous years.

FYI: as PM10 and PM2.5 cannot be tested due to stack conditions, the RTO TPM emission rate is conservatively assumed to equal the emission rate for PM10 and PM2.5

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The 2024 stack test data for RTO-A and Boiler No.1 were reviewed in consultation with SRC. The RTO-A condensable PM value appears consistent with historical results and was not flagged. However, the filterable PM for RTO-A in 2024 (PM-5) may be considered an outlier due to potential sample contamination, as noted by visual evidence of yellow material and abnormal deviation from historical values. Similarly, Boiler No.1 2024 data (PM1) is potentially contaminated and may be excluded from trend evaluations. It is also noted that 2024 experienced fewer days with precipitation /snow during the stack testing period, which could contribute to higher measured particulate concentrations relative to previous years.

FYI: as PM10 and PM2.5 cannot be tested due to stack conditions, the RTO TPM emission rate is conservatively assumed to equal the emission rate for PM10 and PM2.5

reasonsForNoActivities.en: Activities were implemented in a previous year; additional activities are either unnecessary or unfeasible at this time
reasonsForNoActivities.fr: Des activités ont été mises en oeuvre lors d'une année précédente; des activités supplémentaires sont soit inutiles ou irréalisables actuellement
Il n'y a pas suffisamment de capitaux pour exécuter les activités

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Sample variability, batch process.  Less hours old exhaust system was used instead of the Valveless Regenerative Thermal Oxidizers.  More days with precipitation.New data was added to the TPM calcs from air quality modelling (including emissions from truck receiving, grain bin, headhouse, and hammermill).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Sample variability, batch process. Additional estimations were included for process boilers, truck receiving, grain bin, and headhouse (using published emission factors). New data was added to the TPM calcs from air quality modelling (including emissions from truck receiving, grain bin, headhouse, and hammermill).

FYI: as PM10 and PM2.5 cannot be tested due to stack conditions, the RTO TPM emission rate is conservatively assumed to equal the emission rate for PM10 and PM2.5
